name: Apollo A7L
tagline: The first operational lunar surface suit — Apollo 11 through 14
description: First operational lunar surface suit. Hard upper torso, soft-fabric arms and legs, integrated lunar overboots, PLSS backpack with sublimator cooling and LiOH CO₂ scrubber, OPS emergency O₂ cylinder above the PLSS. Operating pressure 3.7 psi pure O₂ — lowest of any flown suit, to maximise mobility for walking in 1/6 g. Flew on Apollo 11 (the first Moonwalk), Apollo 12, and Apollo 13–14. Worn by Buzz Aldrin in the iconic gold-visor lunar surface photo.
best_known_for: The suit worn on Apollo 11–14 — the first to support EVA on another world
